data.content: I can see the project — it's an MSTest project with a failing test (`express` returns `10.00` but the test expects `15.00`). Let me start `dotnet watch test` for hot reload:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. I can start `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start it up.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is up and running. It automatically re-ran after the fix and now shows your failing test:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00`, got `10.00`

**Hot reload is active** — every time you save a file, tests will re-run automatically. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` from `10.00m` to `15.00m` (or adjust the test expectation). The watcher (shell `watch-test`) will pick up the change instantly.
